text.title: Google Ads Account Foundations Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the google-ads-basics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Campaign type MUST match buying intent: Search for high-intent keywords, Shopping for products, PMax only when conversion data ≥30/mo, Display for awareness.
rule.rationale: Wrong campaign type tanks ROAS; PMax with no data eats budget in unobservable buckets.

rule.statement: Each ad group MUST contain 10-20 closely-related keywords (one intent theme). Single-keyword ad groups (SKAGs) are deprecated.
rule.rationale: Match-type modernization makes SKAGs unnecessary; 1-theme groups balance ad-relevance vs management overhead.

rule.statement: Conversion tracking MUST be live AND GA4 events imported before any non-trivial spend.
rule.rationale: Smart bidding fails without conversion signal; spend without conversions is blind.

rule.statement: Every campaign MUST ship ≥3 ad extensions: sitelinks (≥4), callouts (≥4), structured snippets.
rule.rationale: Extensions add 15-30% CTR lift at zero marginal cost.

rule.statement: Billing MUST be linked, payment method verified, and access scoped (admin + manager + read-only roles).
rule.rationale: Billing surprise + over-broad access are the top two non-performance failure modes.
